title: Temperature and Timing Guide
content:
TEMPERATURE & TIMING GUIDE

CANDY TEMPERATURES (use thermometer or cold water test)
234-238°F: Soft Ball (fudge, fondant)
245-248°F: Firm Ball (divinity, caramels)
265-270°F: Hard Ball (taffy)
275-280°F: Soft Crack (butterscotch)
285-290°F: Hard Crack (brittle candy)
310-320°F: Caramel Stage (caramelized sugar)

COLD WATER TESTS
Soft Ball: Forms soft ball that loses shape when removed from water
Firm Ball: Forms firm but plastic ball that holds shape briefly
Hard Ball: Forms hard ball that has lost plasticity
Soft Crack: Forms brittle threads that soften when removed
Hard Crack: Forms brittle threads that remain brittle

OVEN TEMPERATURES
Very Slow: 250-300°F
Slow: 300-350°F
Moderate: 350-400°F
Hot: 400-450°F
Very Hot: 450-550°F

DEEP-FAT FRYING TEMPERATURES
Doughnuts and Fritters: 360-365°F (bread cube browns in 60 seconds)
Croquettes: 365-380°F (bread cube browns in 40 seconds)
French Fried Potatoes: 380-390°F (bread cube browns in 20 seconds)

GENERAL RULES
- Eggs begin to thicken at 144°F
- Milk scalds at approximately 180°F (tiny bubbles form around edge)
- Boiling point decreases approximately 2°F per 1,000 feet elevation
- Pressure cooking at 15 lbs = 250°F regardless of altitude
- Judge griddle heat: drops of water should snap when dropped on smoking-hot surface
